text: Ninety-seven genes have been identified as possible predictors of VO2max trainability, indicating that the capacity to improve cardiorespiratory fitness through training has a substantial genetic component.
Individuals with more positive response alleles (higher gene predictor scores) show greater VO2max improvements from exercise training than those with fewer positive alleles.
Large inter-individual differences exist in VO2max response to the same exercise training program, with genetics being a major contributing factor.
Only 13 of 97 identified genetic variants associated with VO2max trainability have been reproduced by more than two independent research groups, suggesting most findings remain preliminary.
Environmental factors including exercise training program design, diet, and other gene expression mediators interact with genetic variants to influence VO2max trainability.
Current genetic research on VO2max trainability is limited primarily to Caucasian populations, so findings may not generalize across ethnicities.
